channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Tarkov Console Edition ‘Hint’ Found on Icebreaker Map
item.content:encoded:
Escape from Tarkov’s console edition has long been a point of discussion amongst the extraction community. For months, Battlestate Games has openly admitted that the console version is in production, but we don’t at all know to what extent or in what capacity. The leading theory is that it’ll eventually be some kind of ‘Tarkov lite’ version.

When I spoke to Battlestate’s Nikita Buyanov last year, he suggested that they want to retain as much of the core Tarkov feel as possible with a console edition. Now, eagle-eyed players have uncovered another hint to the console edition of Escape from Tarkov on the new Icebreaker map, but it comes with a unique retro twist.

Escape from Tarkov Console Retro Console Reference

Fans probing the new Icebreaker map have uncovered a retro console reference to Escape from Tarkov coming to other platforms. It echoes a joke Nikita Buyanov made a year ago, stating that Escape from Tarkov will come to Game Boy before coming to modern consoles.

In one room on Icebreaker, players have found a replication of a classic PlayStation console alongside a copy of an NES, and in both, there’s a version of Escape from Tarkov waiting to be played.

This is far from being the first time that console replications have popped up in Escape from Tarkov, of course. When you level up your hideout’s rest space far enough, you get a fake PlayStation 4 console with a copy of ‘Call of Call’ sitting beside it. There are also fake copies of Escape from Tarkov on disc littered across the game’s multiple maps.

In October, after the Tokyo Game Show, Nikita was captured on video pledging to deliver a console version of Escape from Tarkov, but again, no prospective release windows were given. In a TarkovTV episode just a few weeks ago, he explained that they’re looking for a partner to help port the game to console, so it’s safe to assume they’re relatively early on in that journey.

Do you want to see Escape from Tarkov come to console? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Fable’s release has been delayed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Bethesda Explains Why Fallout 76’s Infestations is Such an Important Update
item.content:encoded:
On June 2nd, Fallout 76 will receive another chunky update, in the form of ‘Infestations’, an overhaul that periodically implements high-tier, challenging takeovers across the base map of Appalachia. The game’s core locations will be infested with all manner of enemies, and it’s up to players to team up to take them down.

The update introduces a bunch more changes besides just that, including new and gnarly four-star legendary mods, an expansion to the game’s fishing ecosystem, and changes to how teaming up works.

In a recent behind-closed-doors preview, Bethesda Game Studios told us why Infestations is coming.

Infestations Gives Meaning to Smaller Locations

Fallout 76 has an enormous map split up between several biomes, and it’s all comprised of hundreds of points of interest. It’s a staggering thing that can feel overwhelming for a new player, but for a veteran wastelander, the smaller locations typically go ignored, as they don’t offer much beyond a little bit of loot.

This is why Infestations is so important, as it encourages players at the end of their game to revisit locations they perhaps totally forgot about several hundred hours into their character.

Speaking on Infestations, Bethesda Game Studios’ Jon Rush explained:

Infestations is another one of those forays into adding a new layer of experiences on our map. We have a lot of really cool locations in the game that, once you’ve been there once or twice, there’s really not much of a need to go back.

Infestations brings that kind of, ‘what if’ moment. What happens when this place is overtaken by Super Mutants, by Robots, by Scorched? What does that look like?

This is part of a ‘thickening’ attempt to make the base map of Appalachia more interesting and engaging for players. With that being said, Infestations won’t yet reach across the border into Burning Springs.

But this desire to look inward at the base map doesn’t mean there aren’t new things on the horizon. Rush also explained that there are ‘big things, new places, and new stories’ in the pipeline for Fallout 76.

When I spoke to Rush at the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards, he assured me that there’s years’ worth of content lined up for Fallout 76.

Are you looking forward to Infestations arriving in Fallout 76? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Witcher 3’s new expansion will be shown at Gamescom 2026

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.title: New Modern Warfare 4 Map is ‘Crazy’ and Has Over 500 Possible Combinations
item.content:encoded:
There’s only so much you can do to truly evolve Call of Duty multiplayer maps. We’ve seen speeding trains, maps littered with doors, and matches contested in the dark and rain. However, Modern Warfare 4 has a new, constantly-changing idea for a map, and it sounds like one of the most inventive ever.

Infinity Ward has come hot out of the blocks to push Modern Warfare 4 as a new and fresh CoD game. IW claims there will be ‘No BS’ or Silly Skins in Modern Warfare 4, and that the Campaign will ‘return to series’ roots.

We’ve already seen the launch MW4 multiplayer maps, but one, in particular, stands out.

Infinity Ward Has 900 Combinations For MW4 Kill Block Map

Episode 2 of Infinity Ward’s new series about MW4 breaks down the ‘Philosophy of Multiplayer.’

It talks about the natural progression of online multiplayer: Systems, style, gameplay balancing, and, of course, maps.

A huge section of the video is dedicated to the new Kill Block map. Geoff Smith, the game’s Studio MP Creative Director, talked about the ‘next level’ of map design evolution, and one MW4 example that is ‘living’ and ‘keeps changing.’

Kill Block is a Shoot House (originally debuted in 2019’s Modern Warfare) size map, consisting of three ‘slabs.’ The map will change every match, with the slabs being randomly decided before a game.

Smith claimed that IW can do ‘upwards of over 900’ different slabs to feature in MW4, but technical limitations will likely bring this down to 500.

Check out from 4:21 onwards for a full breakdown of Kill Block and the map’s creative concept.

Let’s be honest, we want to see more of this innovation in Call of Duty multiplayer. I think this sounds like a great idea if IW pulls it off, but what do you think? Let us know through the Insider Gaming Discord.

The Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase being the ‘Biggest Live Show Yet,’ and a US Bill to keep games playable after they shut down has passed its first vote, are more hot news stories.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: A US Bill to Keep Games Playable After They Shut Down Has Passed Its First Vote
item.content:encoded:
The California Assembly has passed the Protect Our Games Act by a vote of 43-16, meaning that the bill will move to the California State Senate.

The bill, which was detailed by the Stop Killing Games movement, aims at protecting gamers from having their paid games completely unplayable by publishers after the games ‘shut down’.

The bill’s passage means it will not move to the California State Senate, where it will need to go through an additional, broader vote. If passed, it would “require video game companies to give players advance notice before shutting down server-dependent games and provide a way to keep purchased games playable afterwards, such as offline access, community servers, or another workable option”.

In short, it means that if a gamer has bought a paid game, it must still be playable in some way. In theory, it will likely mean that publishers will have to make their games fully downloaded to play, and publishers might have to offer server services for multiplayer games, though that has not yet been determined.

Unfortunately, though, if the bill is passed, it will only protect games that have been released after January 2027, meaning that any game released before that point won’t be protected. In addition, the law only applies to paid games.

You can watch Chris Ward explaining the votes passing below:

The Stop Killing Games movement in the UK also saw nearly 190,000 signatures signed, exceeding its initialy 100,000 target.

What do you think about the Stop Killing Games movement and the Protect Our Games Act? Let us know your thoughts and join our Insider Gaming Discord.

Read about Call of Duty promising no BS or silly skins in Modern Warfare 4 and an ARC Raiders PvE ‘Rebellion’ map condition being tested in China for more news.

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 8.5 Date & Start Times, New Hero Cyclops & Season 8 End Date
item.content:encoded:
It feels like Season 8 only just began, but it’s already time to start looking towards Season 8.5, and for good reason! We already know that the leader of the X-Men himself, Cyclops, is arriving as the next playable character, and the entire fanbase just cannot wait for him to join the roster.

Season 8 started off well with Devil Dinosaur as the latest Vanguard, and he’s certainly shifted the meta in more ways than one—for better and for worse. Many will be hoping that Cyclops will have a similar effect, though the arrival of a new Season is bound to bring with it a new wave of buffs and nerfs as well.

For details on when Season 8 of Marvel Rivals ends, as well as when Season 8.5 kicks off, you’re in the right place.

When Does Marvel Rivals Season 8 End, & Season 8.5 Start?

As announced in the Season 8 roadmap, the current Marvel Rivals season will end on June 12, with Season 8.5 kicking off right where it left off.

Season 7.5 was the shortest Season we’ve had yet in Marvel Rivals, with the entire thing wrapped up in just four weeks! Well, it seems like NetEase is maintaining that trend, as the first half of Season 8 is just as long!

  • More Insider Gaming: Marvel Rivals Devs Shut Down Anti-Cheat Rumors

It’s honestly miraculous how fast they churn out new heroes and content in Marvel Rivals, especially with the regular events, game modes and skin bundles. That said, with the amount of history running through the Marvel mythos, the development team shouldn’t run out of new content anytime soon—especially if they’re still releasing heavy hitters like Cyclops over a year in!

Marvel Rivals Season 8.5 Start Times

Using other Marvel Rivals seasons as a guideline, it’s reasonable to assume that Season 8.5 will begin on June 12, 2026, at 3 AM PT.

A new season is always the most exciting time to play Marvel Rivals, and the arrival of Cyclops is bound to be one of the best we’ve seen yet. The hype is already through the roof after the Season 8 trailer that introduced him, so to finally play as him is bound to be a special moment for countless Marvel fans out there.

If you’re desperate to play as the new hero day one, here’s the exact moment Season 8.5 should go live across the world:

Everything We Know About Cyclops So Far

The details surrounding Cyclops remain fairly vague, as it’s still unclear which role he will play in Marvel Rivals.

Many are hoping that the X-Men leader will fill in as a Vanguard, with many crying out for more appealing tank options. It wouldn’t get more enticing than Cyclops, but there’s still a chance he could arrive as a Duelist, with some even predicting him to be a Strategist!

  • More Insider Gaming: Will Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Have Zombies?

Until we know his role for sure, it’s tough to predict how he’ll play in-game, though one thing that’s practically a guarantee is that his kit will undoubtedly revolve around his optic beams. He’s seen blasting countless Alchemax robots in the original trailer, and he does try to protect Moon Girl, further fuelling the Vanguard suspicions.

Time will shortly tell, as a character trailer will release a few days before Season 8.5 begins. Expect details to begin to arrive on June 8, including information regarding the latest buffs and nerfs to the rest of the roster.

Are you excited for Season 8.5 of Marvel Rivals? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Will Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Be On XBOX Game Pass?
  • All Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Pre-Order Prices, Editions & Rewards
  • Every Map in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4—All New Maps & How Many There Are

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase Will be ‘Biggest Live Show Yet’
item.content:encoded:
The annual Summer Game Fest Showcase has become the show of shows: The Wrestlemania and Super Bowl of gaming! Now we know that Geoff Keighley’s announcements can sometimes be hyped, but the SGF creator is promising the biggest show to date.

Geoff Keighley built up Highguard, and yes, it was a disaster. However, we’ve had tons of amazing moments across Summer Game Fest and The Game Awards, and while the 2025 SGF edition was good, the 2026 version could be spectacular.

Geoff Keighley Teasing ‘Biggest Summer Game Fest Live Show’

Keighley has taken to X to really amp up the hype for the Summer Game Fest Showcase 2026. There’s a chance that appetites will already be whetted, thanks to the Sony State of Play event on June 2—which could also be mega.

But on May 31, here’s what Geoff had to say:

‘Just 5 days until our biggest #SummerGameFest live show yet. See you live on Friday at 5p ET / 2p PT / 10p BST / 11p CEST / 6a JST.’

You can find out more details about the full Summer Game Fest 2026 calendar right here.

There are currently a plethora of huge announcements expected to happen in the next few days, but it’s unclear which are being saved for the Sony State of Play presentation and Summer Game Fest Showcase 2026.

Make sure you know how to watch the Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase, as our guide has a complete run-through of the dates, start times around the world, and more.

What are you hoping to see during the Summer Game Fest Showcase 2026? Do we think Highguard’s mistakes won’t be repeated? Let us know your thoughts and join our Insider Gaming Discord.

Read about Call of Duty promising no BS or silly skins in Modern Warfare 4 and an ARC Raiders PvE ‘Rebellion’ map condition being tested in China for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

item.title: Every Team Qualified for the Esports World Cup 2026 Valorant Tournament
item.content:encoded:
The teams for the Esports World Cup (EWC) 2026 Valorant tournament have been set following the conclusion of regional qualifiers for the event. EWC 2026 Valorant will run from July 2-12.

Eleven teams made their way through a gruelling gauntlet to join EWC 2025 Champions, Team Heretics, alongside the winners of VCT Stage 1 in Pacific, Americas, and China, with Vitality taking the VCT EMEA Stage 1 spot as runners-up.

Esports World Cup 2026 Valorant qualified teams:

  • Heretics
  • Paper Rex
  • G2
  • EDward Gaming
  • Vitality
  • Gentle Mates
  • Karmine Corp
  • BBL Esports
  • MIBR
  • 100 Thieves
  • NRG
  • Rex Regum Qeon
  • Global Esports
  • Nongshim RedForce
  • XLG Esports
  • AG.AL International*

*AG.AL International is the umbrella name for All Gamers/Anyone’s Legend used at the Esports World Cup.

Fnatic fall short again in EWC EMEA qualifiers

In EMEA, Karmine Corp ran the entire qualifier from Phase 1, reserved for the worst-performers of Stage 1, all the way to the event. The roster defeated Eternal Fire and Fnatic in the main bracket to qualify for EWC, a rare ray of hope for the roster, which finished joint-last in VCT EMEA Stage 1.

Gentle Mates also made it through the upper-bracket, defeating Liquid and Masters London Qualified FUT to make the event. BBL took the final spot, defeating NAVI 3-0 after NAVI had eliminated Fnatic to make the lower final.

The event is the third international event Fnatic will miss in 2026, joining VCT Masters Santiago and London in a harrowing year so far for the Fnatic organization.

NRG take the long way to Paris

In Americas, MIBR took revenge for their VCT Stage 1 defeat to regional rivals, Leviatán, with Erick ‘aspas’ Santo leading his men to a 30- victory to make EWC 2026.

100 Thieves joined them, recovering from painfully missing out on VCT Masters London to take down FURIA and LOUD to claim their spot at the event.

Valorant Champions 2025 winners, NRG, typically did not take the easy route to Paris, falling in their opening contest to LOUD before recovering against FURIA, before knocking out the Stage 1 runners-up Leviatán in the lower semis.

A rematch with LOUD awaited NRG, but the match proved to be a no-contest as NRG ran out 13-10, 13-7, 13-5 winners to secure the final Americas spot at the event, joining VCT Stage 1 winners G2.

RRQ returns the international fray with EWC qualification

The Pacific qualifier saw Rex Regum Qeon (RRQ) return to an international for the first time in 2026, after attending Valorant Champions and the Toronto Masters in 2025.

The roster took a narrow 3-2 series win against Gen.G to make the event, with Global Esports qualifying by the same scoreline against Valorant Masters Santiago winners Nongshim RedForce.

Nongshim took the final spot of the qualifier, defeating DRX and Gen.G, who will be heartbroken to so narrowly miss EWC 2026.

XLG make yet another international event

With only two spots to contend for, the Chinese EWC would prove to be a particularly brutal bloodbath. No team suffered more than Dragon Ranger Gaming. The VCT Stage 1 third-placed team finished joint-last in the qualifier, without winning a single map.

Fellow Valorant Masters London qualified roster, XLG, had no problem reaching yet another international, defeating FunPlus Phoaenix, TYLOO, and Titan Esports Club to take the first spot available.

JD Gaming, TYLOO, Trace Esports, FunPlus Phoenix, and Titan Esports Club all fell by the wayside in the Lower Bracket, leaving only All Gamers as the best of the rest, claiming their spot at EWC 2026, playing as AG.AL International.

Let us know who you think will win the EWC 2026 Valorant tournament on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For other news, see the details of Insider Gaming’s Showcase!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/esports-world-cup-2026-valorant-teams/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For June 1, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, June 1, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Gemstone
  • Hint 2: Variety of quartz
  • Hint 3: Purple in color

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Chalcedony
  • Facet
  • Sapphire
  • Gemstone
  • Garnet
  • Topaz
  • Aquamarine
  • Peridot
  • Citrine

Today’s Contexto Answer For June 1, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Amethyst

Amethyst is a purple variety of quartz that is often used in jewellery.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on June 2.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
